title: Visa guidance for performers and support staff :: Immigration New Zealand
content:
## Visa guidance for performers and support staff
Performers and support staff for festivals or entertainment acts must apply for a NZeTA (New Zealand Electronic Travel Authority) or a specific visitor visa to travel to New Zealand.

## Check if you need to apply for a visa
You can request an NZeTA (New Zealand Electronic Travel Authority) to travel to New Zealand for short-term live shows if:
- you are from a visa waiver country, or
- you are from China or a Pacific Islands Forum country and you are travelling from Australia with an eligible Australian visa.
Visa waiver countries and territories
Chinese and Pacific Island visitors travelling from Australia
- a letter of invitation or support from an approved promoter, and
- no criminal convictions.
A NZeTA should be requested as soon as possible, and at least 72 hours before the intended travel date.
New Zealand Electronic Travel Authority (NZeTA)

## If you need to apply for a visa
You may need to apply for a visa if you:
- are not from a visa waiver country
- are not from China or a Pacific Islands Forum country and travelling from Australia with an eligible Australian visa, or
- have character concerns.
The correct visa depends on the type of event, for these visas you must be invited or supported by an approved promoter.
Arts and Music Festival Visitor Visa
Short-term Entertainment Act Visitor Visa

### Criminal convictions
If you have criminal convictions you must apply for a visitor visa even if you are being promoted by an approved promoter or festival.
Visitor visa applications involving criminal convictions often take longer to process due to the need for additional verification.
You may be required to provide a police certificate if you have been convicted of an offence and sentenced to a prison term. You may also require a special direction, depending on the length of your prison sentence.
A special direction will also be required if you have been removed, excluded or deported from another country.
You should apply as early as possible to make sure your visa is processed in time. We recommend applying at least 4 weeks before your intended travel date.
Fees, decision times and where to apply
Visitor visa and NZeTA wait times
We may be able to escalate the process of making a decision of any visitor visas.
Escalation for urgent applications

### Provide all information
You must provide all the relevant information we need to assess the visa application.
Providing evidence and documents to support your visa application
